justification: All standard ADR sections present (Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives Considered). Crash-recovery lock behavior explicitly described in Consequences (both the positive case of connection-drop release and the negative case of hung process holding connection). All three alternatives listed: Redis SETNX, ZooKeeper, heartbeat table. Context clearly explains the multi-node expansion problem and why coordination is needed. No invented information — all content traces back to the MJ input. ADR is fully readable as a standalone document.

# ADR-001: Distributed Coordination for Nightly Batch Job via PostgreSQL Advisory Locks

**Status:** Accepted

**Date:** 2026-03-18

---

## Context

The nightly reconciliation job previously ran on a single node with no coordination mechanism. As the system expands to multiple nodes, concurrent execution of the batch job must be prevented — only one node should run the job per night.

The coordination mechanism must be reliable enough to handle the expected load (one job per night, one coordina
